rulings.Case Title: GIANCARLO MARANGHI VS. BENNY CHETCUTI JR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside The Dismissal And Judgment, And For Leave To Allow Pltf To File The Fourth Amended Compplaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF GIANCARLO MARANGHI's Motion To Set Aside The Dismissal And Judgment, And For Leave To Allow Plaintiff To File The Fourth Amended Complaint. Denied. (CCP § 473(b).) Plaintiffs are not entitled to mandatory relief pursuant to CCP § 473(b). "Courts have held the mandatory provision is inapplicable to . . . failure to file an amended complaint after a demurrer has been sustained with leave to amend." (Gotschall v. Daley (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 479, 483-84.) Plaintiffs are not entitled to discretionary relief under a mistake of law; Plaintiffs' former counsel's mistake was not reasonable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LUIS ADOLFO MARQUEZ et al VS. PG & E et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANTs INDUSTRIAL CREW, LLC and GOLDEN GATE DISPOSAL & RECYCLING COMPANY's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Motion granted. PG&E has not met its burden to show that the settlement was not made in good faith. (City of Grand Terrace v. Superior Court (1987) 192 Cal.App.3d 1251, 1261.) The settlement is within ballpark based on the Settling Defendants' proportionate liability for Plaintiff's fall. The evidence presented by PG&E is insufficient to negate Plaintiff and Plaintiff's employer's potential comparative negligence or to establish that the settlement figure is "grossly disproportionate" to a reasonable estimate of the Settling Defendants' liability. (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BANK OF THE WEST VS. BODY DETAILS, INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For New Trial
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANTs BODY DETAILS, INC. and ROBERT OZAKI's Motion For New Trial. Denied. First, the motion does not comply with California Rule of Court 3.1113, which requires a memorandum to be filed in support of a motion. Second, the Declaration of Mitchell Sens is inadmissible because it states it was made under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of Florida. In order for a declaration to be admissible, it must be made under the laws of the state of California. (Kulshrestha v. First Union Commercial Corp. (2004) 33 Cal.4th 601, 618.) Third, Defendants set forth no basis for setting aside the Court's previous order granting summary judgment in favor of Plaintiff. Defendants cannot establish that there is "newly discovered evidence¿ which [they] could not, with reasonable diligence, have discovered and produced" earlier. (CCP sec. 657.) Defendants have conducted no discovery since being served with the complaint in August 2012. (Declaration of Marc T. Cefalu, par. 3.) Additionally, Defendants do not identify a specific "accident or surprise" that would require vacating or modifying the Order. (CCP sec. 657.) Lastly, the Court already considered the issue of standing in ruling on the motion for summary judgment and determined "the unambiguous language of the contracts state that Trinity is 'a division of Bank of the West.' (Declaration of James Roach, Exs. 1, 5 - 7.)" (March 7, 2014 Order Granting Summary Judgment.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 2.550(d) and (e) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MERVYN SILBERBERG VS. UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summ Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANT UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment is granted. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that plaintiff requested that defendant exhaust the insurance policy limits to settle the Liljestrand litigation and defendant complied with plaintiff's request. Defendant discharged its obligations under the policies. When the Liljestrand litigation was settled, Ms. Curran stated that she did not plan to file a claim. Defendant did not act in bad faith by settling the only known claim against its insured. In addition, plaintiff has waived its right to sue defendant over its handling of the Liljestrand case because defendant complied with plaintiff's request to exhaust the policies. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. BALTAZAR AVALOS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Initial Responses To Plaintiff'S Discovery - Special Interrogatories (Set One) And For An Order Deeming The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Plaintiff'S Request For Admissions (Set One) As Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s Motion To Compel Initial Responses To Plaintiff'S Discovery - Special Interrogatories (Set One) And For An Order Deeming The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Plaintiff'S Request For Admissions (Set One) As Admitted Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, for reasons set forth in Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s motion, which is unopposed. Defendant Baltazar Avalos is ordered to respond to Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories (Set One), without objection, within 15 days from the date the written Order on this motion. It is further ordered that the truth of the matters specified in Plaintiff's Request for Admissions (Set One) propounded on Defendant Baltazar Avalos is deemed admitted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD CHAE VS. DORON WEINBERG
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 17, DEFENDANT DORON WEINBERG's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. "Factual innocence is an element of a legal malpractice cause of action stemming from representation in a criminal action." Wilkinson v. Zelen (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 37, 45. In this case, paragraph 10 of the complaint indicates that plaintiff is still serving the requirements of his sentence. Paragraph 12 states that plaintiff's appeal is currently pending. Accordingly, plaintiff has not been exonerated and cannot allege factual innocence. A stay is not appropriate under Coscia v. McKenna & Cuneo (2001) 25 Cal.4th 1194. CCP 340.6 was amended after that decision was issued and plaintiff now has two years after exoneration to commence an action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: AZAR AFSHARIPOUR VS. SAKS FIFTH AVENUE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT SAKS & COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for negligent retention is sustained without leave to amend. This cause of action is preempted by the worker's compensation exclusivity provision. (Lab. Code sec. 3600(a).) There is a "tripartite system for classifying injuries arising in the course of employment" for purposes of determining whether that injury is covered by the exclusive remedy provisions of the workers' compensation law. (Fermino v. FedCo, Inc. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 701, 713.) "First, there are injuries caused by employer negligence or without employer fault that are compensated at the normal rate under the workers' compensation system. Second, there are injuries caused by ordinary employer conduct that intentionally, knowingly or recklessly harms an employee, for which the employee may be entitled to extra compensation under section 4553. Third, there are certain types of intentional employer conduct which bring the employer beyond the boundaries of the compensation bargain, for which a civil action may be brought." (Id. at 714.) The negligent retention cause of action arises from employer negligence, thus falling under the first prong. (See Coit Drapery Cleaners Inc. v. Sequoia Ins. Co. (1993) 141 Cal.App.4th 1595, 1606, holding that a negligent supervision claim would "be barred¿by the workers' compensation laws, since [an employee] may not sue for its allegedly negligent or improper supervision.") The authorities cited by Plaintiff do not mandate a different result. Unpublished Federal District Court opinions are not binding. Further, none of the California cases cited discuss preemption of negligent supervision or retention causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 2.550(d) and (e) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GERALD LAURENCE TREBESCH VS. FALL LINE CAPITAL, LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay All Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANTs FALL LINE CAPITAL, LLC, ERIC O BRIEN and CLAY MITCHELL's Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay All Proceedings. Granted in part and denied in part. Granted as to the first, second, third, fourth, fifth, eleventh, twelfth, and thirteenth causes of action. These causes of action are "arising out of, in connection with, or relating to" the Limited Liability Company Agreement (the "GP Agreement"). (Declaration of Eric O'Brien, Ex. A, par. 9.2.) Although Fall Line GP is not a named defendant in this matter, Plaintiff seeks to recover under the GP Agreement, and therefore he is precluded from repudiating the arbitration clause in the contract entered into with Fall Line GP. (Rowe v. Excline (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 1276, 1287.) The GP Agreement is not unconscionable. Denied as to the sixth, seventh, eighth, ninth, and tenth causes of action. These causes of action arise from Plaintiff's employment with Fall Line and do not seek damages under the GP Agreement. The arbitration provision in the TriNet Agreement does not govern these claims. The TriNet Agreement defines covered disputes as those arising out of or relating to Plaintiff's employment with TriNet. (O'Brien Decl., Ex. B, sec. 9(a).) The TriNet Agreement states that TriNet is Plaintiff's co-employer for administrative purposes. (Id. at sec. 1.) Plaintiff's claims do not arise or relate to administrative issues. Alternatively, the scope of the arbitration provision in the TriNet Agreement is ambiguous. "A contract may be explained by reference to the circumstances under which it was made, and the matter to which it relates." (Civ. Code § 1647.) The evidence establishes that Defendants informed Plaintiff that the TriNet Agreement does not govern the terms of Plaintiff's employment with Fall Line. (Declaration of Gerald Trebesch, Ex. A.) The litigation in this Court is stayed pending the outcome of arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 2.550(d) and (e) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. THAI MING CHIU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANT THAI CHIU's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is continued to June 24, 2014, on the court's motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. THAI MING CHIU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANTs THAI CHIU and KAMAN LIU's Joint Motion For Sanctions. The motion is continued to June 24, 2014, on the court's motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: WHITE OAK GLOBAL ADVISORS, LLC VS. KENNETH MASTERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To File Portions Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 16, PLAINTIFF WHITE OAK GLOBAL ADVISORS, LLC's Motion To File Portions Of Complaint Under Seal. The motion is granted. The motion meets the requirements of CRC 2.550 et seq. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 2.550(d) and (e) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
